divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020
story_angle: eyewitness dispatch from the scene with vivid sensory detail
headline: A Somber Journal of London's Plague-Wrought Silence
deck: An eyewitness account reveals the haunting echoes of a life once vibrant, now succumbing to despair.
article:
In the year of our Lord, eighteen hundred and ninety, I find myself traversing the streets of London, a city once resplendent in its vibrancy, now enveloped in a pallid fog that clings to the remnants of its former glory. The stench of decay hangs heavy in the air, a macabre perfume that mingles with the dampness of a gray sky. Each step upon the cobblestones feels laden with the weight of despair, as I pass by dilapidated mansions, their opulence eroded into forlorn echoes that speak of lives extinguished and aspirations undone.

As I venture deeper into the heart of this moribund metropolis, I am met with a haunting stillness that pervades the streets. The bustling markets that once thrived with vibrant bartering and laughter now stand silent, their stalls abandoned, a cruel testament to the specter of the Great Plague that has wreaked havoc upon our beloved society. It is here, in this desolation, that I am compelled to reflect upon the tapestry of loss that has enveloped us. Those who once thrived have been swept away, leaving behind only whispers of their existence etched into the fabric of this suffering city.

Faded posters of hope, once affixed to crumbling walls, now seem but a grim reminder of humanity's fragility. Their promises of rebirth and renewal dissolve into the mist, as if mocking our collective yearning for salvation in a world turned against us. I read the slogans, once bold and brimming with optimism, their ink now a pale shadow of its former vibrancy. "Together we shall overcome," they proclaimed, yet here we stand, fragmented and alone, grappling with a reality that offers no solace.

With each passing day, I bear witness to the slow march of decay, not merely in the architecture of our city but within the very souls of its remnants. The echoes of laughter and camaraderie have long since faded, replaced by the melancholic sighs of those who remain. We are left to ponder the depth of our loss, to grapple with the specter of mortality that has cast its shadow upon us all, as we navigate the ruins of our civilization with a heavy heart, haunted by the question of what remains when hope has withered like the autumn leaves beneath our feet.
author: Foreign Bureau

oped.title: Silence? Nay, the Plague Has Merely Muzzled the Rabble
oped.author: Lord Percival Graves
oped.body: The laments over London's 'plague-wrought silence' reek of sentimental rot. With nine souls in ten erased since the 2020 scourge, the city's streets finally permit a gentleman to hear his own thoughts without the ceaseless braying of costermongers and factory drudges. This hush is no tragedy but overdue sanitation. The dead have done what Parliament never dared: they have culled the vulgar excess that once passed for vitality. Let the mourners wail; the rest of us shall enjoy the first honest peace this century has known.

classifieds.text: Seeking skilled domestic help for a quaint Victorian estate in the heart of London. Duties include housekeeping, meal preparation, and care for a small garden of rare blooms. Applicants must demonstrate prior experience and a dedication to maintaining a pristine environment. References from previous employers appreciated. Pay commensurate with experience.

Join us for an exquisite evening of classical music and theatrical performances at the Grand Pavilion this Saturday eve. Revel in the splendid melodies of accomplished musicians and the grace of our finest actors, as we celebrate the resurgence of art in these trying times. Tickets available at the door or through the local ticketing office.

Introducing the elegance of the steam carriage! Travel in style with our newest model, complete with plush seating and polished brass fittings. Ideal for those seeking a comfortable ride through the newly rebuilt countryside. Inquire at the local station for a demonstration and to schedule your first journey. Limited availability—reserve yours today!

The East London Society invites all residents to attend our monthly gathering at the Town Hall. Engage in discussions about rebuilding our community post-plague, share resources, and foster connections with fellow survivors. Light refreshments will be served. Together, we can pave a brighter future for our children. An RSVP is preferred, but walk-ins are welcome!

panels.caption: The diarist notes: 'No costermongers cry their wares; only the wind rattles the shutters.'
A beadle stamps the last parish roll: 'Ninety souls claimed. Mark it paid in full.'
The final line reads: 'At least the damn fog has somewhere to sit.'

joke.setup: The evening gazette devotes its full column to London's plague-wrought silence,
joke.punchline: a curious choice, given that nine readers in ten now keep their opinions underground.
joke.text: The evening gazette devotes its full column to London's plague-wrought silence, a curious choice, given that nine readers in ten now keep their opinions underground.

sponsor_ads.headline: Silence Requiem: The Noiseless Piano
sponsor_ads.body: Experience the exquisite melodies of emptiness with our patented Noiseless Piano. Perfect for those mourning the plague's theft of sound, now you too can play music that echoes only in your mind.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Brought to you by the Society for the Preservation of Forgotten Tones.

sponsor_ads.headline: Plague-Era Perfume: 'Essence of Absence'
sponsor_ads.body: Drape yourself in the delightful aroma of nostalgia with our latest fragrance, a blend of ash and faded memories. For the discerning individual who prefers to smell like yesterday’s tragedy.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Whispering Walls: Your Home’s Inaudible Companion
sponsor_ads.body: Introduce a new layer of eerie elegance to your abode with our wall treatment that absorbs sound! Let your home become a sanctuary where even the echoes of despair cannot reach you.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Requires no maintenance; simply ignore it.

sponsor_ads.headline: Now Available: Plague-Safe Coffee
sponsor_ads.body: Enjoy your morning brew with none of the bothersome company of the living! Our exclusive blend is infused with the loneliness of a plague emptied London.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not responsible for unsolicited thoughts or visions of the departed.
